Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position after DOWN from start
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Player area (rows 13-22, cols 13-25):")
for i in range(13, 23):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][13:25]}")

# Also check red path
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in after:
    if n['id'] == 7:
        print(f"Red path pixels: {n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

2. DOWN: rows 14-18, cols 14-18 (current)

Looking at the white corridor layout from earlier:

- Rows 19-25: cols 32-36 (center horizontal)
- Rows 26-37: cols 20-24 (left horizontal)
- Rows 39-41: cols 42-46 (right horizontal)
- Rows 42-48: cols 20-24 (left horizontal)
The player needs to navigate from the top-left to the bottom-right. Let me try moving RIGHT to enter the white corridor.

</parameter>
</function>
